task_objective: Separate the QAM's ephemeral displayed-game selection from the persisted selected_game preference so unrelated settings mutations cannot move the panel to another game.
tests_added: Store tests prove setDisplayedGame changes only selectedGame and hydrateDisplayedGame seeds only an empty display.
State invariant tests prove applySettings never changes the displayed game.
Refresh-selection tests cover valid live selection precedence, cold persisted preference selection, stale live selection fallback, and first-game fallback.
Startup and QAM hydration tests cover persisted-preference seeding without overwriting an existing display.
Initial-content tests prove settings and history request exceptions degrade independently and game-list synchronization still establishes a valid selection.
Settings mutation regressions prove both global Automatic Sync and per-game sync responses leave displayed game A unchanged when the backend preference remains B.
design_decisions: selectedGame is ephemeral QAM view state; settings.selected_game remains the persisted fallback preference and is written only by the deliberate dropdown onGameChange path.
applySettings no longer derives selectedGame from settings. The old ludusaviState test expectation was inverted because it encoded the coupling that caused this bug, rather than desired behavior.

The onGameChange optimistic assertion now expects settings.selected_game to remain at its previous value until the RPC is applied. The related queued-resolution assertion also remains at the previous value until an earlier mutation leaves the serialized queue; these expectation changes describe the new persistence boundary rather than masking a failure.
Refresh application reads selectedGame live from the store. A valid live selection suppresses the persisted preference, while a stale live selection does not discard a valid persisted preference.
Both startup and QAM hydration route through hydrateDisplayedGame, so only a cold display is seeded and reopening the QAM preserves the last viewed game.
Settings and history fetches use Promise.allSettled and convert thrown requests into failed RPC-status values, allowing game-list synchronization and selection recovery to continue.
No backend or dependency changes were required.

results.automated: Task-specific red-green checks passed through the implementation commits, culminating in 313 frontend tests plus TypeScript typecheck. Every commit hook also passed Ruff, formatting, ty, 838 Python tests at 88.69% coverage, frontend supply-chain checks, build, and the complete frontend suite. The orchestration quality gate is run once more after this documentation commit before the round-complete marker is written.
results.scope: Frontend-only; no py_modules backend file changed.
results.release: No release, tag, push, or manual merge is part of this implementation round; approved orchestration finalization owns those actions.
results.on_device: Deferred and not claimed as passed. Steam Deck verification must confirm that per-game sync, Automatic Sync, and notification toggles do not move the displayed game; manual dropdown selection persists; Steam-context auto-selection still works; two no-game QAM reopens keep the last viewed game; and pulled qam_opened logs report a sensible displayed selected_game value with the documented open-time timing.
